note: Registry + tick + deterministic detectors over other swarms' dashboards (cron seed -> tick). Consumed as a mix dep.

name.description: Object name in the topology (default: scope).

registry.description: Observed swarms: name -> {dashboard_url, token_env, repo}. token_env is the NAME of an env var (x-secret contract, section 14.2.1) — never a token literal. Hot-add a swarm to watch by patching this map.

token_env.description: Env var NAME holding the dashboard bearer token; omit for loopback dashboards.

repo.description: GitHub org/repo of the observed swarm, linked in alert cards.

thresholds.description: Detector tuning overrides: stall_minutes (10), error_burst_count (5), error_burst_window_s (60), pool_saturated_s (120).

cooldown_minutes.description: Minimum minutes between two alerts of the same (swarm, type) — the anti-storm dedupe window.

alert_conversation_id.description: Telegram conversation the alert cards go to (sender's conversation_id).

tick_sources.description: Nodes allowed to trigger tick (fail-closed allowlist; normally just cron).

read_sources.description: Nodes allowed to call status/get_dashboard/get_events (fail-closed allowlist; the diagnosis agent).

custom_detectors.description: Third-party detector modules to run alongside the built-ins. Boot-time only — read once at init/1, never re-read, NEVER hot-mutable: loading in-process code is the operator's explicit allowlist, not a tuning knob. Each entry is either a bare module (a fully-qualified module string/atom, applied to every observed swarm) or {module, swarms: [name, ...]} to scope it to specific swarms (swarms omitted/empty = global). Modules are vendored via gsp (swarmidx:<scope>/<pkg>@<ver>) and digest-pinned by the notary; an unresolvable module or one missing detect/2 raises at boot (fail-closed) rather than being silently skipped.

swarms.description: Swarm names this detector runs against; omit or empty for all observed swarms.

sender.description: Topology object that receives alert cards (default: sender).

escalate_to.description: Topology agent that receives each (cooldown-deduped) alert as a diagnosis task (fase 3; unset = no escalation). Trust surface — not hot-mutable.

client_opts.description: Extra adapter opts (timeout_ms, fake pid in tests).

deliver_fn.description: Injectable delivery seam to the sender (tests only; default engine ObjectServer).
